key_points: The story presents the life of an ordinary middle-class couple
Mme Loisel’s dissatisfaction is the root cause of the conflict
The necklace acts as the main turning point of the story
Loss of the necklace leads to ten years of hardship
The ending reveals a strong element of irony
characters: Mme Loisel – Ambitious, proud, and dissatisfied with her social position
Mr Loisel – Responsible, patient, and self-sacrificing
Mme Forestier – Represents wealth and social status
important_events: Mr Loisel’s sacrifice for the dress
Borrowing of the necklace from Mme Forestier
Loss of the necklace after the ball
Life of struggle and repayment of debt
literary_devices: Situational Irony – A fake necklace causes real suffering
Realism – Focus on everyday life and social pressure
moral_values: Desire for status can destroy peace of mind
True happiness does not depend on wealth
exam_tips: Explain how the necklace drives the plot
Use the ending to explain irony
Connect themes with examples from the story
